description_long: This object consists of several metal guard rails, each constructed from tubular steel with a painted finish. The rails are supported by vertical posts and have horizontal bars at the top and bottom, with evenly spaced vertical rods in between for added rigidity. The guard rails are painted in blue, black, and silver, with some showing a matte or slightly glossy finish. The bases of the posts are flat, suggesting a surface-mounted or base plate anchoring method. The ends of the rails are blunt and squared off, with no visible flaring or special end treatments. These guard rails are commonly used in urban environments to delineate pedestrian areas, protect from vehicular intrusion, or guide foot traffic.
description: A set of tubular metal guard rails in blue, black, and silver, with vertical posts and horizontal bars, surface-mounted with blunt ends.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length and color variety of the guard rails, with clear visibility of the vertical posts and horizontal bars.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the rails and the alignment of the vertical posts, with the bases visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back structure of the rails and the arrangement of the posts, with color differences still apparent.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the depth and spacing between the rails, as well as the base plates anchoring each post.
